content.verdict: Eilert Smith is the most quietly luxurious city hotel in Norway — a 12-room boutique stay inside a restored 1937 modernist warehouse on Stavanger harbour, with three-Michelin-star RE-NAA on the ground floor. Designed by Anemone Wille Våge in muted Norwegian palettes, with brass, oak and original concrete, it is intentionally small and grown-up. For a honeymoon, it pairs perfectly with the Lysefjord and Pulpit Rock day trip, and is the right city base before flying north to the Arctic.
content.best_room: Book one of the harbour-view King rooms or the corner Suite with private terrace. Rooms have walk-in showers, deep tubs, custom-made beds and original 1937 windows framing the harbour. The corner Suite is the splurge — extra natural light from two sides and a private terrace overlooking the water.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival in Stavanger
itinerary_7_nights.description: Fly into Stavanger Sola (SVG) and transfer the 20 minutes to the harbour. Check into your King room, take a slow walk through the white wooden lanes of Gamle Stavanger and an aperitif at the hotel bar before settling in.

itinerary_7_nights.title: RE-NAA tasting menu
itinerary_7_nights.description: Spend the day at leisure — a coffee at Talor & Jørgen, a long lunch in the harbour, and the evening at three-Michelin-star RE-NAA on the ground floor. Walk back to your suite the moment dinner ends.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Pulpit Rock hike
itinerary_7_nights.description: Drive or join a guided trip to Preikestolen (Pulpit Rock) — the iconic 604-metre cliff over the Lysefjord. The hike is 4 hours round-trip. Back at the hotel, a long bath and an in-room dinner.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Lysefjord cruise
itinerary_7_nights.description: Board a small electric Rødne fjord cruise from the Stavanger harbour into the Lysefjord, sailing under Pulpit Rock from below. Lunch on board, return for an afternoon at leisure and a casual supper at Sabi Omakase or Bølgen & Moi.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Sola Strand & beach day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Drive 25 minutes to the wide Sola and Borestranden beaches for a Nordic-style beach day with fjord-cool air and long sandy walks. Return for sauna access and a tasting menu of Norwegian seafood.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Old Stavanger & street art
itinerary_7_nights.description: Walk Norway's largest preserved wooden quarter (173 white houses) in the morning, lunch at the historic Renaa Matbaren, then explore Stavanger's Nuart-festival street art trail. Final dinner at the hotel bar with langoustine toast.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Farewell breakfast
itinerary_7_nights.description: Slow harbour breakfast, a final espresso on the terrace, then transfer back to Sola airport. Many couples connect onward to Bergen by sea or fly north to Tromsø for a Northern Lights extension.

caveats: Only 12 rooms — books out 4-6 months ahead, especially around Stavanger food festivals.
City hotel, not a beach property — Sola Strand is 25 minutes by car.
RE-NAA tasting menus require booking 6-8 weeks ahead.
Some rooms face the inland street rather than the harbour — specify view at booking.

faqs.question: Good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Yes — small, design-led and serious about food. It is the most grown-up boutique city stay in southern Norway and pairs naturally with a Lysefjord day trip.

faqs.question: Best time to visit?
faqs.answer: May to September for long days, open Pulpit Rock trail and warmer harbour walks. April and October are atmospheric and quieter.

faqs.question: Book in advance?
faqs.answer: 4-6 months ahead for harbour-view rooms; 6-8 weeks ahead for RE-NAA dinners.

faqs.question: Adults-only?
faqs.answer: No, but the scale (12 rooms) and Michelin-led tone make it an adults-leaning stay.

faqs.question: Best room type?
faqs.answer: Harbour-view King for value, corner Suite with private terrace for the splurge.

faqs.question: How to get there?
faqs.answer: Fly into Stavanger Sola (SVG), 20 minutes by taxi or airport bus. Direct flights from London, Amsterdam, Copenhagen and Oslo.
